description: Shared objects and tokens for craft project metadata. These are used by social.craftsky.feed.post and multiple social.craftsky.project.* detail lexicons.

project.description: Wraps a craft project's shared fields (#projectCommon) and optional craft-specific fields (#details, open union). The wrapper exists so shared fields live in one place (common) while per-craft specialisations are added additively via the union. See ADR 001.

common.description: Shared project fields that apply across all crafts.

details.description: Optional craft-specific fields. Open union — new crafts add new variants here without breaking existing records.

projectCommon.description: Project fields shared across every craft. A post with project.common present but no project.details is a valid craft-tagged project post without specialised fields — useful when craftType references a craft that has no #details lexicon yet.

craftType.description: The craft this project belongs to. Required — a project post without a craft type is meaningless. knownValues are tokens; new crafts can be added to feed.defs without breaking old clients.

status.description: Whether the project is in progress or finished at the time this post was created. Snapshot — not a mutable lifecycle flag. knownValues are tokens so new statuses (e.g. planned, frogged) can be added without breaking old clients.

title.description: Optional project name, e.g. 'Hitchhiker Shawl' or 'Linen Summer Dress'. Clients showing grid/card views should fall back to truncated text when title is absent.

duration.description: Free-text description of how long the project took, e.g. '3 weeks', 'a weekend', '6 months of evenings'. Deliberately unstructured — crafters describe duration informally. Not range-queryable.

materials.description: Materials used in the project, as free-form tags. Indexed for search, e.g. 'show me all projects using linen'. Structure per material is intentionally free-form because every craft uses different material descriptors.

colors.description: Primary colors used in the project, normalized by clients for search and filtering. knownValues are open suggestions; clients may write other bounded color strings when needed.

designTags.description: Visual design, motif, or aesthetic tags for the project, normalized by clients for cross-craft search and filtering. knownValues are open suggestions; clients may write other bounded design strings when needed.

tags.description: Structured search tags. Composer responsibility to normalise to ASCII kebab-case (pattern ^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$). AppView search materialisation also merges hashtag facets from post text and faceted project metadata, so this field is not required to duplicate every visible hashtag.

pattern.description: Optional reference to the pattern used. Every field optional — 'Simplicity 8265' (name only), 'https://ravelry.com/patterns/library/hitchhiker' (URL only), or both plus difficulty are all valid.

name.description: Pattern name or visible pattern hashtag, e.g. 'Simplicity 8265', 'Hitchhiker Shawl', or '#hitchhiker'. Useful when there is no URL (e.g. a physical pattern), or alongside a URL as a display label. Rich-text annotations for this string live in nameFacets.

nameFacets.description: Byte-range annotations over 'name'. Used for pattern hashtags and future links/mentions in the visible pattern name string.

difficulty.description: Pattern difficulty as rated by the designer. A property of the pattern, not the post — self-drafted or free-formed projects should leave this empty. knownValues are tokens.

designer.description: The person or people who designed the pattern. Free text so physical patterns, indie designers, and multiple-designer credits can be represented without account references. Rich-text annotations for this string live in designerFacets.

designerFacets.description: Byte-range annotations over 'designer'. Mention facets may notify credited Craftsky accounts.

publisher.description: The person, company, or entity that published the pattern. Optional and free text because pattern publishing differs across crafts and eras. Rich-text annotations for this string live in publisherFacets.

publisherFacets.description: Byte-range annotations over 'publisher'. Mention facets may notify credited Craftsky accounts.

toyHobby.description: Toys, hobby objects, games, dolls, and play items.

pet.description: Pet clothing, bedding, toys, and accessories.

medical.description: Medical, adaptive, support, or care-related projects.

component.description: Motifs, swatches, parts, trims, blocks, appliques, or other components.

quilt.description: Quilts as a primary project category.

alteration.description: Mending, repair, refashioning, or adjustment of an existing item.

costume.description: Costume, cosplay, theatre, or fancy-dress projects.

other.description: Intentionally uncategorized or not covered by the current vocabulary.

botanical.description: Botanical, leaf, plant, or garden-inspired design.

lettering.description: Design featuring lettering, words, initials, or typography.

gauge.description: Structured stitch gauge measured over a stated distance. Rows are optional because many crafters record only stitch gauge.

stitches.description: Number of stitches measured across the gauge swatch.

rows.description: Number of rows or rounds measured, when recorded.

measurement.description: Measurement distance for the gauge swatch, interpreted with unit.

unit.description: Measurement unit for the gauge swatch.
